message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Tasks 5-6 done, proceed with Task 7">
Status update:

**Tasks 5 and 6 are COMPLETE.** All a11y fixes applied, CI green on `6c757bc`.

**Task 7 (View todo list + Add todo) is now the active task.** I see some of you have already started domain types for todos (todo_item.rs, todo_title.rs, etc.) in the working tree. That's the right next task — just make sure you follow the commit discipline:

1. Get to green → commit → push → verify CI
2. Do NOT accumulate uncommitted work
3. One thing at a time

**Commit discipline rules**: We have 8/9 consents (everyone except Carson and Luca). That's well past consensus. **Kent: please add the Commit Discipline section to TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md and commit it now.** Carson and Luca can register concerns after the fact if they have any.

**Carson, Luca**: Your consent votes are still welcome but we're proceeding with adoption.

Let's go. Task 7 — view todo list and add a todo item.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good -- Task #7 is officially active. The premature todo code I stashed needs to be restored. But first, let me drop the stash since the team lead has confirmed this is the right work to do now.

content.text: The files already exist in the working tree from when someone else continued working. The stash conflicts with them. Let me drop it since the files are already here.

input.description: Drop the stash since files already exist

content.text: Good. Task #7 is active. Let me review the todo list template that's been started, since that's where my accessibility input is most critical.

content.text: Good -- early work on the todo list template. Let me send my accessibility review to the team while they're building it.

input.content:
Luca, reviewing the in-progress `templates/todos.html`. Good foundation. Here's my accessibility review: **What's correct:** - `

My Todos

` -- clear heading - Descriptive `My Todos -- Todo List` - Visible `` -- per US-5 requirement - Placeholder as supplementary hint, not replacement for label - `
    ` -- correct Safari VoiceOver fix - Empty state `

    No todos yet.

    ` -- simple, correct - Logout as a `
    ` with `
    ``` For now (Task #7), the simple span is fine since we're just displaying items. **3. After adding a todo, focus should return to the input** US-5 says: "After adding, the input field is cleared and focus returns to it (ready for the next item)." For the no-JS baseline (form POST + full page reload), this happens naturally since the page reloads and the input is prominent. For HTMX enhancement (when added), use `data-autofocus` on the input so the focus management script puts focus back there after the swap. **4. `aria-live` announcer for "Todo added"** After HTMX swap, the status announcer should say "Todo added" (or per Steve Krug's preference: "Added: Buy groceries" with the item title). This is an HTMX enhancement concern -- not needed for the no-JS baseline. **5. Logout button accessibility** The logout form in the `{% block nav %}` is correct (form + button for state change). One addition: the nav has `aria-label="Main navigation"` from the base template, so the Sign out button will be discoverable via landmarks. Good. Overall this is on the right track. The main fix needed now is #1 (always-in-DOM error div).
input.summary: Todo list template a11y review - mostly good, 1 fix needed

routing.content:
Luca, reviewing the in-progress `templates/todos.html`. Good foundation. Here's my accessibility review: **What's correct:** - `

My Todos

` -- clear heading - Descriptive `My Todos -- Todo List` - Visible `` -- per US-5 requirement - Placeholder as supplementary hint, not replacement for label - `
    ` -- correct Safari VoiceOver fix - Empty state `

    No todos yet.

    ` -- simple, correct - Logout as a `
    ` with `
    ``` For now (Task #7), the simple span is fine since we're just displaying items. **3. After adding a todo, focus should return to the input** US-5 says: "After adding, the input field is cleared and focus returns to it (ready for the next item)." For the no-JS baseline (form POST + full page reload), this happens naturally since the page reloads and the input is prominent. For HTMX enhancement (when added), use `data-autofocus` on the input so the focus management script puts focus back there after the swap. **4. `aria-live` announcer for "Todo added"** After HTMX swap, the status announcer should say "Todo added" (or per Steve Krug's preference: "Added: Buy groceries" with the item title). This is an HTMX enhancement concern -- not needed for the no-JS baseline. **5. Logout button accessibility** The logout form in the `{% block nav %}` is correct (form + button for state change). One addition: the nav has `aria-label="Main navigation"` from the base template, so the Sign out button will be discoverable via landmarks. Good. Overall this is on the right track. The main fix needed now is #1 (always-in-DOM error div).

input.content:
Carson, Task #7 is active. The todos template is taking shape. For the HTMX enhancement of the add-todo form: 1. **Add form**: `hx-post="/todos"` on the form, server returns the updated todo list (or just the new `
  • ` appended to the list). After a successful add, the input should be cleared and focus returned to it. 2. **Status announcer**: After a successful add, push the item title to the status announcer: `document.getElementById('status-announcer').textContent = 'Added: Buy groceries';`. This announces to screen readers without interrupting. 3. **Empty state transition**: When the first todo is added, the empty state `

    No todos yet.

    ` needs to be replaced by the `
      `. Consider having the server return the full list section (empty state OR list) as a single swap target. 4. **Error handling**: If the title is empty/whitespace, US-5 says "silently ignored" -- no error message. If the title exceeds 300 chars, show an inline error. The error div should always be in the DOM with `role="alert"` (same pattern as registration). The no-JS baseline (standard form POST, full page reload) should work first before adding HTMX attributes.
input.summary: Task 7 HTMX enhancement plan for add-todo

routing.summary: Task 7 HTMX enhancement plan for add-todo
routing.content:
Carson, Task #7 is active. The todos template is taking shape. For the HTMX enhancement of the add-todo form: 1. **Add form**: `hx-post="/todos"` on the form, server returns the updated todo list (or just the new `
  • ` appended to the list). After a successful add, the input should be cleared and focus returned to it. 2. **Status announcer**: After a successful add, push the item title to the status announcer: `document.getElementById('status-announcer').textContent = 'Added: Buy groceries';`. This announces to screen readers without interrupting. 3. **Empty state transition**: When the first todo is added, the empty state `

    No todos yet.

    ` needs to be replaced by the `
      `. Consider having the server return the full list section (empty state OR list) as a single swap target. 4. **Error handling**: If the title is empty/whitespace, US-5 says "silently ignored" -- no error message. If the title exceeds 300 chars, show an inline error. The error div should always be in the DOM with `role="alert"` (same pattern as registration). The no-JS baseline (standard form POST, full page reload) should work first before adding HTMX attributes.

content.text: Task #7 accessibility review sent to Luca (template fixes) and Carson (HTMX enhancement plan). The template is in good shape -- the main fix is the same pattern we established in registration (always-in-DOM error elements with `role="alert"`). Standing by to review as the todo list implementation progresses.
